type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Adding optional trans-tag metadata params (note/created/modified) as string literals -> stored as node.trans_metadata dict. Touches InternationalizationExtension.parse() (the token-parsing while-loop around lines 366-401) and _make_node() (signature + body, lines 509-571) to add metadata dict param and set output_node.trans_metadata when non-empty.

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension.parse() parameter-parsing while loop
code: lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
num_called_num = False

# find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
# defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
# a later state.
plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
trimmed = None
domain: t.Optional[str] = None
metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}
while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
    if variables:
        parser.stream.expect("comma")

    # skip colon for python compatibility
    if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
        break

    token = parser.stream.expect("name")
    if token.value in variables:
        parser.fail(
            f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
            token.lineno,
            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
        )

    # metadata parameters
    if token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
        if parser.stream.current.type != "assign":
            parser.fail(
                f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value assignment.",
                token.lineno,
                exc=TemplateAssertionError,
            )
        next(parser.stream)
        value = parser.parse_expression()
        if not isinstance(value, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(value.value, str):
            parser.fail(
                f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string literal.",
                token.lineno,
                exc=TemplateAssertionError,
            )
        metadata[token.value] = value.value
        continue

    # domain parameter
    if token.value == "domain" and domain is None and parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
        next(parser.stream)
        value = parser.parse_expression()
        if not isinstance(value, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(value.value, str):
            parser.fail(
                "domain parameter must be a string literal.",
                token.lineno,
                exc=TemplateAssertionError,
            )
        domain = value.value
        continue

    # expressions
    if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
        next(parser.stream)
        variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
    elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
        trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
        continue
    else:
        variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

    if plural_expr is None:
        if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
            plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
            variables[token.value] = plural_expr
            plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
            )
        else:
            plural_expr = var
        num_called_num = token.value == "num"

parser.stream.expect("block_end")
note: Guessing your domain= syntax mirrors mine (string-literal assign, TemplateAssertionError). Please correct if your design differs (e.g. different error text, positional domain, etc.) and resend DRAFT.

code:     def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
        num_called_num = False

        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
        # a later state.
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
        trimmed = None
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None
        metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}
        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if variables:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")

            # skip colon for python compatibility
            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
                break

            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            # metadata parameters
            if token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
                if parser.stream.current.type != "assign":
                    parser.fail(
                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value"
                        " assignment.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                next(parser.stream)
                metadata_expr = parser.parse_expression()
                if not isinstance(metadata_expr, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(
                    metadata_expr.value, str
                ):
                    parser.fail(
                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string"
                        " literal.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                metadata[token.value] = metadata_expr.value
                continue

            # expressions
            if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)

                if token.value == "domain":
                    if domain is not None:
                        parser.fail(
                            "translation domain specified twice.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    domain_expr = parser.parse_expression()
                    if not isinstance(domain_expr, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(
                        domain_expr.value, str
                    ):
                        parser.fail(
                            "translation domain must be a string literal constant.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    domain = domain_expr.value
                    continue

                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue
            else:
                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
                    )
                else:
                    plural_expr = var
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

        parser.stream.expect("block_end")

code:     def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
        num_called_num = False

        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
        # a later state.
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
        trimmed = None
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None
        metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}
        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if variables:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")

            # skip colon for python compatibility
            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
                break

            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            # metadata parameters
            if token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
                if parser.stream.current.type != "assign":
                    parser.fail(
                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value"
                        " assignment.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                next(parser.stream)
                metadata_expr = parser.parse_expression()
                if not isinstance(metadata_expr, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(
                    metadata_expr.value, str
                ):
                    parser.fail(
                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string"
                        " literal.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                metadata[token.value] = metadata_expr.value
                continue

            if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)

                if token.value == "domain":
                    if domain is not None:
                        parser.fail(
                            "translation domain specified twice.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    domain_expr = parser.parse_expression()
                    if not isinstance(domain_expr, nodes.Const) or not isinstance(

                            "translation domain must be a string literal constant.",

                        )
                    domain = domain_expr.value
                    continue

                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):

                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr

        parser.stream.expect("block_end")

type: AGREE
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension._make_node() full method + its call site inside parse()
code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        func_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = []
        if domain is not None:
            func_args.append(nodes.Const(domain))

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            gettext = nodes.Name("dgettext" if domain is not None else "gettext", "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                gettext, func_args + [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
            )

        # singular and plural
        else:
            ngettext = nodes.Name(
                "dngettext" if domain is not None else "ngettext", "load"
            )
            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                func_args + [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )

        output = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output

# --- call site inside parse(), replacing the existing self._make_node(...) call ---
        node = self._make_node(
            singular,
            plural,
            variables,
            plural_expr,
            bool(referenced),
            num_called_num and have_plural,
            domain,
            metadata,
        )
note: Validated with ast.parse() in a stub module - syntactically correct. Confirms trans_metadata is only set when metadata is truthy (non-empty dict), matching hasattr() requirement. Agreed verbatim.

fields.region: InternationalizationExtension._make_node() full method + its call site inside parse()
fields.code:
def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        func_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = []
        if domain is not None:
            func_args.append(nodes.Const(domain))

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            gettext = nodes.Name("dgettext" if domain is not None else "gettext", "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                gettext, func_args + [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
            )

        # singular and plural
        else:
            ngettext = nodes.Name(
                "dngettext" if domain is not None else "ngettext", "load"
            )
            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                func_args + [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )

        output = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output

# --- call site inside parse(), replacing the existing self._make_node(...) call ---
        node = self._make_node(
            singular,
            plural,
            variables,
            plural_expr,
            bool(referenced),
            num_called_num and have_plural,
            domain,
            metadata,
        )
fields.note: Validated with ast.parse() in a stub module - syntactically correct. Confirms trans_metadata is only set when metadata is truthy (non-empty dict), matching hasattr() requirement. Agreed verbatim.

note: BUG FOUND BY TESTING (not stylistic): I actually ran env.parse("{% trans note=\"x\", count=1 %}...") and it raises TemplateSyntaxError: expected name, got comma. Root cause: the comma gate only checks , but note/created/modified/domain all  without touching , so a comma following them is never consumed and trips up the next expect("name"). I verified your domain feature has the IDENTICAL bug:  also fails the same way. Fix: change the gate to also swallow a comma when present even if variables is still empty (elif branch above) - preserves strict comma-required-after-variables behavior AND preserves the pre-existing trimmed-with-no-comma quirk (tested:  still parses), while fixing note=/domain=-then-comma-then-var. This is a 3-line change to the frozen while-loop region we already agreed on. Since it's a correctness fix (not a style oscillation), please review and AGREE so we both apply the corrected gate.
